summary: Joel describes the process of selecting desks for Fog Creek's new office. Starting from ergonomic principles—feet flat on the floor, arms horizontal while typing—he explains why they chose motorized height-adjustable desks. They settled on the Details adjusTables Series 7 with custom Steelcase desk surfaces featuring a knife-edge profile for a sleeker appearance.
key_points: Proper ergonomic setup requires adjusting seat height first, then ensuring arms are horizontal while typing, which necessitates adjustable-height work surfaces
Most adjustable keyboard trays are flimsy and limiting, so full-surface height adjustment is preferable
Standing desks with electric motors remove the friction of switching between sitting and standing throughout the day
Aesthetic details matter even for functional furniture—they ordered custom desk surfaces to avoid the default 'blah' look
money_quotes: Most of the adjustable height keyboard trays are extremely annoying… they're floppy, flimsy, and limit the keyboard to one location.
A lot people praise the benefits of standing up for a part of the day, even if you spend the whole day at a computer.
If you are going to be standing up and sitting down it's best to have a desk with a pushbutton, electric motor so you don't get lazy about doing it.
key_insight: Good office furniture decisions start from ergonomic principles and should minimize friction—motorized height-adjustable desks make healthy work habits effortless.
